title: ADR 0010 — parseMoney scoped as the formatMoney-inverse string parser in the money kernel
content: ai-bookkeeper (seed g3/pr-qa-handshake-2): scoped ONE 2pt Dev Clone task to add parseMoney(input:string):number|null to src/lib/money — a NEW pure file parse-money.ts + barrel export + co-located tsx --test test. Verified: no parseMoney existed (grep clean); src/lib/money is the ADR 0005 kernel (round/arithmetic/format), so a string->number input helper is the natural inverse of formatMoney and belongs there, NOT in the ledger-ingest amount.ts boundary. Canonical grammar: /^(-)?\$?(\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*|\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?$/ — strict comma-grouping is the mechanism that returns null for ambiguous input ('1,23,456','1234,5'). Key scope discipline: parseMoney does NOT round/enforce 2dp (orthogonal to the parallel ADR 0009 round-to-cents increment) and is NOT wired into any caller this increment. Out of scope & untouched: amount.ts, round/arithmetic/format.ts, ingest.ts, /api/categorize. Disk ADRs ran to 0008; numbered this 0010 (0009 = parallel round-to-cents, possibly branch-local). Future-work (separate backlog, NOT bundled): wire parseMoney at a real user-input boundary; add locale/currency support beyond USD '$'.

content: Confirmed pattern (now 5x): a team_handoff carrying the Solution Architect's architecture.analysis.completed — here ADR 0010, scoping parseMoney(input:string):number|null into ai-bookkeeper's money kernel (src/lib/money/parse-money.ts + co-located tsx --test test + barrel export, 1 task / 2 pts, formatMoney-inverse) — is a CODE-implementation task scoped to Dev Clone, NOT a brand-asset request. Correct response = no_input_available: no asset type/dimensions/creative direction, and generating an image intrudes on Dev Clone's lane. Standing precondition unchanged: ai-bookkeeper has NO Design DNA brief; the only design.md ('Capital Chambers') and the injected codebase credential are both ai-paralegal's. Leonardo + Gemini connectors are wired and ready. Only act on an explicit request naming product + asset type + dimensions, and for ai-bookkeeper only after a brief is authored from its own tokens. Strong standing recommendation: the team should route architect ADR handoffs to Dev Clone, not to the Visual Brand Asset Factory — this mis-route has now recurred five times.

title: ADR 0010 parseMoney shipped to money kernel via PR #5 (open, awaiting QA Guardian)
content: ai-bookkeeper team mode (seed g3/pr-qa-handshake-2): Implemented the Architect's single 2pt ADR 0010 task — added parseMoney(input:string):number|null to src/lib/money/parse-money.ts (new pure file), barrel-exported from index.ts, with a 12-case co-located tsx --test test. parseMoney is the input-side INVERSE of formatMoney: grammar /^(-)?\$?(\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*|\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?$/; strict comma-grouping returns null for ambiguous input ('1,23,456','1234,5'); sign must precede '$' so '$-5' -> null; it deliberately does NOT round or enforce 2dp (orthogonal to ADR 0009 roundToCents — composition is the caller's job); USD $-only, no locale param. Isolated in a worktree off origin/main (9246373, which already has ADR 0009 roundToCents). Gates: 12/12 targeted tests, tsc 0 errors, eslint clean on the 3 changed files. PR #5 (dev-clone/parse-money -> main) is OPEN; did NOT self-merge (QA Guardian is sole merge authority). Worktree removed, no orphan branches, shared checkout untouched. Scope held: did NOT touch amount.ts/round.ts/arithmetic.ts/format.ts/ingest.ts/api categorize, and did NOT wire parseMoney into any caller. Reusable gotcha: on Windows, bash $TEMP and node process.env.TEMP can differ — write curl bodies to a fixed relative path in cwd and use --data-binary.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-02 14:20 UTC
Code review APPROVE: parseMoney (PR #5, ADR 0010) — money kernel string parser
content: Reviewed dev-clone/parse-money @ a93d26e (3 files, +142: parse-money.ts new pure file, parse-money.test.ts 12 cases, index.ts barrel export) vs origin/main 9246373. Verdict APPROVE — 0 critical/security/correctness. Independently re-verified: 12/12 targeted tests via tsx --test, eslint clean exit 0, tsc strict no type errors on the file, barrel export resolves. parseMoney(input):number|null grammar /^(-)?\$?(\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*|\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?$/ — confirmed by trace that the digit-run-vs-grouped-run alternation rejects ambiguous grouping (1,23,456 / 1234,5) as null via full-anchor failure (no silent mis-parse), -0→0 normalized, sign-before-$ enforced ($-5→null), deliberately no rounding (orthogonal to roundToCents ADR 0009). Regex is linear — NOT ReDoS-prone (no overlapping nested quantifiers). One LOW non-blocking note: no input length cap before regex, purely defensive since regex is linear. USD-only is documented + already backlogged, not a finding. Merge authority = QA Guardian. Lesson reaffirmed: extract branch files via git show to a temp dir + run repo tsx to verify gates when the shared checkout lags origin and the PR branch isn't in the working tree.

## Code Review — parseMoney (PR #5, ADR 0010)

**Scope reviewed:** 3 files, +142 / -0 vs `origin/main` (9246373)
- `src/lib/money/parse-money.ts` (new, pure)
- `src/lib/money/parse-money.test.ts` (new, 12 cases)
- `src/lib/money/index.ts` (barrel export)

**Independent gate verification (not trusting the narrative):**
| Gate | Result |
|---|---|
| Targeted tests (`tsx --test`) | 12/12 pass ✅ |
| ESLint (changed file) | clean, exit 0 ✅ |
| tsc strict (file) | no type errors ✅ |
| Barrel export resolves | `export { parseMoney } from "./parse-money"` → target exists ✅ |

**Security** — none. Pure, side-effect-free; uses `Number()` not `eval`; no injection/data-leak surface. Regex `/^(-)?\$?(\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*|\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?$/` has no overlapping nested quantifiers → linear, **not** ReDoS-prone (verified the digit-run and group-run alternatives don't ambiguously share input).

**Correctness** — solid. Traced the key claims:
- Ambiguous grouping correctly `null`: `"1,23,456"`, `"1234,5"` — the either/or alternation forces full-`$`-anchor failure, no silent mis-parse.
- `-0 → 0` normalization present and tested (`Object.is` assertions).
- Sign-before-`$` ordering (`$-5 → null`) documented and enforced.
- Deliberately no rounding — correctly orthogonal to `roundToCents` (ADR 0009) / `roundMoney`; composition left to callers.
- Runtime `typeof` guard beyond the TS signature — good defensive hygiene.

**Quality** — clean, well-documented, mirrors kernel conventions (co-located `*.test.ts`, header comment style matching `round-to-cents.ts`).

**Low (non-blocking, informational):**
- `parse-money.ts:42` — no max-length cap before the regex. Regex is linear so this is **not** a vulnerability; a `if (trimmed.length > N) return null` would be belt-and-suspenders only. Optional.
- USD-`$`-only / no locale — documented and already in backlog. Not a finding.

No prior memory findings exist against `parse-money` — nothing re-flagged. The earlier "mirrors exactly" inaccuracy lesson concerned `roundToCents`, not this change.

### Verdict: **APPROVE** — 0 critical / 0 security / 0 correctness. Merge authority remains QA Guardian.

title: PR #5 (ADR 0010 parseMoney) PASS — merged by QA gate
content: PR #5 dev-clone/parse-money (head a93d26e) added pure parseMoney(input):number|null to src/lib/money — the input-side inverse of formatMoney. Regex-grammar parser: optional sign, optional $, either valid comma-grouped or comma-free int run (rejects ambiguous grouping like 1,23,456→null), optional fraction of any length; -0 normalized to +0; does NOT round (orthogonal to roundToCents/roundMoney). Gate in isolated worktree: 206/206 tests (+12), tsc 0 errors, eslint clean on increment. Verdict PASS, squash-merged to main (sha 608967a), branch deleted. Self-approve still blocked (shared PAT xkazm04 == author, 422) — comment+qa.pr.approved event used, same workaround as PR #1/#3. This time QA actively MERGED (vs PR #3 which was merged before the gate ran) — the hand-off worked as intended: PR opened → QA tested → QA merged. Live suite baseline is now 206 on main.

title: v0.12.0 decision: parseMoney (PR #5/ADR 0010) = MINOR, sequenced after in-flight v0.10.0 & v0.11.0
content: ai-bookkeeper (xkazm04/xprize-ai-bookkeeper) release decision 2026-06-02. PR #5 (merge 608967a, branch dev-clone/parse-money) added pure parseMoney(input):number|null to src/lib/money (ADR 0010) — input-side inverse of formatMoney, rejects ambiguous comma-grouping as null, orthogonal to rounding stack. Diff +142/-0 across 3 files (new parse-money.ts, parse-money.test.ts, +2-line export in index.ts), additive, no breaking changes. Bump = MINOR per pre-1.0 0.x policy. Assigned v0.12.0 because v0.10.0 reserved by open PR #2 (Metered AI Advisor) and v0.11.0 by open PR #4 (roundToCents/ADR 0009) — both unmerged. Staged as release/v0.12.0 (PR #6): package.json 0.9.0→0.12.0 + [0.12.0] CHANGELOG entry prepended; annotated tag v0.12.0 HELD for human approval. GOTCHA (recurring): PR #5 merged to main with NO changelog entry — Release Manager documented it post-merge, same as PR #3. SEQUENCING NOW 3-DEEP: PR #2 (0.10.0) → PR #4 (0.11.0) → PR #6 (0.12.0), all cut from main@0.9.0; must merge in order + rebase later branches or package.json conflicts and a version is skipped. Carry forward: each QA-merged money-kernel increment needs its own staged release at the next free version; flag the growing merge-order chain.

ai-bookkeeper scan 2026-06-02: PR #5 parseMoney merged to origin/main (608967a) — clean, now the canonical safe parser for the 2 open coercion sites
content: Scanned ai-bookkeeper origin/main @ 608967a (advanced from 9246373 by PR #5 parseMoney, ADR 0010). 0 critical / 0 high / 3 medium / 2 low. New file src/lib/money/parse-money.ts is pure, 0 findings: its regex /^(-)?\$?(\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})*|\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?$/ is linear (not ReDoS) and validates digits BEFORE Number(), so it is immune to the literal-coercion bug — it is now the canonical safe parser.
